title: Differentiation of Human iPSCs into Forebrain Neurons
author: Xylena Reed

steps.title: Grow human iPSCs on Matrigel-coated plates in E8 medium
steps.instruction: Grow human iPSCs on Matrigel-coated plates in E8 medium.

steps.title: Plate iPSCs so that each iPSC...
steps.instruction: Plate iPSCs so that each iPSC line occupies one well of a 6-well plate.

steps.title: Allow cultures to reach 95–100% confluency before initiating differentiation
steps.instruction: Allow cultures to reach 95–100% confluency before initiating differentiation.

steps.title: Plan to feed differentiation cultures using...
steps.instruction: Plan to feed differentiation cultures using 5 mL media per well per day.

steps.title: Start differentiation (Day 1):
steps.instruction: Start differentiation (Day 1):

steps.title: Aspirate E8 medium from iPSCs at 95–100% confluency
steps.instruction: Aspirate E8 medium from iPSCs at 95–100% confluency.

steps.title: Add 5 mL per well of N3 medium supplemented with
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL per well of N3 medium supplemented with: Dorsomorphin: 1.5 µM SB

steps.title: 10 µM 00:10:00 Incubate for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: 10 µM 00:10:00 Incubate for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Feed daily with N3 + dual-SMAD inhibitors (Days 1–11):
steps.instruction: Feed daily with N3 + dual-SMAD inhibitors (Days 1–11):

steps.title: Replace media daily with fresh N3 + dorsomorphin + SB
steps.instruction: Replace media daily with fresh N3 + dorsomorphin + SB

steps.title: Use 5 mL per well
steps.instruction: Use 5 mL per well. 00:10:00 Incubate for 10 minutes per day. ‱ Expected result: cells transition into neuroepithelial-like morphology over time.

steps.title: Dual-SMAD inhibitor preparation (500 mL N3)
steps.instruction: Dual-SMAD inhibitor preparation (500 mL N3): Factor | Final Concentration | Stock | Volume for 500 mL %s %s (For 300 mL N3: dorsomorphin 45 µL, SB431542 300 µL)

steps.title: Feed daily with N3 only (Days 12–15):
steps.instruction: Feed daily with N3 only (Days 12–15):

steps.title: From Day 12 to Day 15,...
steps.instruction: From Day 12 to Day 15, replace media daily using N3 medium only (no dorsomorphin, no SB431542).

steps.title: Use 5 mL per well
steps.instruction: Use 5 mL per well. 00:10:00 Incubate for 10 minutes per day.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.instruction: Step 4: Feed daily with N3 + RA (Days 16–19): 1. From Day 16 through Day 19, replace media daily using N3 medium supplemented with RA: Retinoic acid: 0.05 µM 00:10:00 Incubate for 10 minutes per day. ‱ Protect RA-containing media from light. ‱RA addition guideline: stock RA = 10 mM Example: 1 µL RA stock in 200 mL N3

steps.title: Coat plates with Poly-L-ornithine (Day 17):
steps.instruction: Coat plates with Poly-L-ornithine (Day 17):

steps.title: On Day 17, coat two 6-well...
steps.instruction: On Day 17, coat two 6-well plates per original 6-well plate (splitting 1:2).

steps.title: Add 1 mL PLO per well
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL PLO per well.

steps.title: Incubate overnight at 37°C
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at 37°C. Overnight ‱ Ensure full well coverage.

steps.title: Coat plates with Fibronectin + Laminin (Day 18):
steps.instruction: Coat plates with Fibronectin + Laminin (Day 18):

steps.title: Wash wells 3× with DPBS
steps.instruction: Wash wells 3× with DPBS.

steps.title: Prepare Fibronectin/Laminin coating solution in DPBS
steps.instruction: Prepare Fibronectin/Laminin coating solution in DPBS: Fibronectin/Laminin coating solution (10 mL total) %s %s

steps.title: Add coating solution to wells (1 mL per well)
steps.instruction: Add coating solution to wells (1 mL per well).

steps.title: Incubate 4 hours to overnight at 37°C
steps.instruction: Incubate 4 hours to overnight at 37°C.

steps.title: Wash wells 3× with DPBS
steps.instruction: Wash wells 3× with DPBS.

steps.title: Leave DPBS in the final wash...
steps.instruction: Leave DPBS in the final wash until ready to plate cells (do not allow wells to dry). 04:00:00 4 hours - Overnight ‱ Do not allow coated plates to dry.

steps.title: Split cells 1:2 (Day 19):
steps.instruction: Split cells 1:2 (Day 19):

steps.title: Aspirate media and rinse cells with...
steps.instruction: Aspirate media and rinse cells with 2 mL DPBS per well.

steps.title: Add 1 mL Accutase per well
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL Accutase per well.

steps.title: Incubate 9 min at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate 9 min at room temperature (check under microscope; extend if needed).

steps.title: Add 1 mL DPBS, gently pipette...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL DPBS, gently pipette to detach cells, and transfer to a 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Rinse well with an additional 1...
steps.instruction: Rinse well with an additional 1 mL DPBS and combine in tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 150 × g for 4 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 150 × g for 4 min.

steps.title: Aspirate supernatant
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 2 mL N3...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 2 mL N3 + RA + ROCK inhibitor.

steps.title: Plate cells 1:2 by adding 1...
steps.instruction: Plate cells 1:2 by adding 1 mL cell suspension into each prepared well containing 4 mL N3 + RA + ROCK inhibitor. ‱ Avoid harsh trituration; neuronal progenitors are fragile. ‱ ROCK inhibitor improves survival after replating.

steps.title: Feed daily with N4 (Days 20–28):
steps.instruction: Feed daily with N4 (Days 20–28):

steps.title: Starting Day 20, replace media daily using N4 medium
steps.instruction: Starting Day 20, replace media daily using N4 medium.

steps.title: Continue daily feeding until Day 28 (or longer if desired)
steps.instruction: Continue daily feeding until Day 28 (or longer if desired).

steps.title: Use 5 mL per well
steps.instruction: Use 5 mL per well. 00:10:00 Incubate for 10 minutes per day. ‱ Expected result: increasing neurite extension and neuronal network formation.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.instruction: Step 9: Freeze or transfer neurons (Day 24 or later): 1. From Day 24 onward, neurons may be: frozen down replated for assays maintained in culture (feeding every other day) ‱ Freezing guideline: Freeze 1 well of a 6-well plate into 1 vial Thaw 1 vial into 2 wells

steps.title: Neural induction morphology should be visible by Day 7–11
steps.instruction: Neural induction morphology should be visible by Day 7–11. After replating (Day 19–20), cells should recover and begin forming extended processes. By Day 24–28, neuronal networks should be apparent. Expected neuronal marker expression includes: SOX1 / PAX6 (early neural progenitors) TUJ1 (βIII-tubulin) and MAP2 (neuronal identity) Forebrain-associated markers depending on line/conditions (e.g., FOXG1 )

steps.title: Problem
steps.instruction: Problem: Cells detach during early differentiation Cause: harsh media changes Solution: add media slowly along the wall of the well, avoid touching the monolayer

steps.title: Problem
steps.instruction: Problem: Poor survival after splitting Cause: over-incubation in Accutase or insufficient ROCK inhibitor Solution: monitor detachment closely and include ROCK inhibitor during replating

steps.title: Problem
steps.instruction: Problem: Weak neurite outgrowth Cause: growth factors degraded or media not changed consistently Solution: prepare fresh BDNF/GDNF aliquots, ensure daily N4 feeding

steps.title: N3 Medium (Neuronal Differentiation Medium) 50%...
steps.instruction: N3 Medium (Neuronal Differentiation Medium) 50% DMEM/F12 50% Neurobasal™** Penicillin-Streptomycin: 1× B-27™ minus vitamin A: 0.5× N2 supplement: 0.5× GlutaMAX™: 1× NEAA: 1× β-mercaptoethanol: 0.055 mM Insulin: 1 µg/mL Store at 4°C up to 1 week.

steps.title: N4 medium (Neuronal Maturation Medium) N4 = N3 plus
steps.instruction: N4 medium (Neuronal Maturation Medium) N4 = N3 plus: BDNF: 2 ng/mL (25 µg/mL stock, 40 µL added to 500 mL) GDNF: 2 ng/mL (10 µg/mL stock, 100 µL added to 500 mL) Retinoic acid (RA): 0.05 µM (10 mM stock, 2.5 µL added to 500 mL) ‱ Add growth factors and RA fresh when possible.
